task.title: Setting preferences for client certificate authentication
task.shortdesc: Use the Client Certificates preference page to specify preferences for client certificate authentication.

taskbody.context: With client certificate authentication, you can authenticate with remote systems by using a security device such as an integrated circuit card (like a smart card). relies on the Java Cryptographic Service Provider (Java CSP) for the retrieval of the certificates. The certificates are used solely for client certificate authentication. All updates to the certificates are outside the scope of .

steps.stepsection: To set up client certificates on your workstation:

dlentry.dd: Specify the name of the security provider as provided by the vendor that supplies the cryptography software that is used to access the certificates.

Specify the keystore type that the Java CSP uses for retrieving the certificates that are stored in the Microsoft Cryptography API (MS-CAPI) keystore. Use the name of the keystore that the security provider gives.

Specify the hostIdMappings OID. Do not change the value that is specified on the preferences page from the default value of 1.3.18.0.2.18.1 unless instructed otherwise by the systems administrator. The hostIdMappings extension (Object Identifier 1 3 18 0 2 18 1) is an IBM extension that is also available for public use. The security software on the remote system (such as RACF) automatically maps a valid certificate to the RACF user ID that is provided in the extension. Changing this value might cause the certificate authentication to fail.

step.cmd: To ensure that each keystore has a unique alias, select the check box.

info.note: Select this option only when you are using the Sun MSCAPI Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) Provider and only when the aliases are not unique. Do not select this option for other JCE providers.

step.cmd: To filter certificates by key usage, select one or more of these checkboxes that are listed under :
step.info: Digital signature Non-repudiation Key encipherment Data encipherment Key agreement Certificate signing CRL Signing Encipher only Decipher only For information about the types of key usage, see Key usage extensions and extended key usage.You can filter by key usage alone or combined with filtering by hostIdMappings OID.

link.linktext: Key usage extensions and extended key usage
